description:
Advances: specled.compiler_context, specled.mix_tasks

Deliverable:
Per refined-plan D6/D7 and specled.decision.tracer_manifest_merge_on_flush:
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/context.ex: FIX Context.load/1 default manifest path — currently Path.join(compile_path, ".mix/compile.elixir") resolves UNDER ebin/ and loads 0 modules (empirically verified; correct sibling path loads 79). New default: Path.join([Path.dirname(compile_path), ".mix", "compile.elixir"]) — dirname-based so an explicit compile_path: override still finds its sibling .mix/. ADD Context.from_mix_project/1: derives app/env/compile_path/manifest_path/build_path from Mix.Project + Mix.env, accepts keyword overrides, delegates to load/1; the ONLY Context function consulting Mix globals (load/1 stays Mix-free). Moduledoc: name it the production entry-point constructor.
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex: build context via @doc false default_context(root) — returns Context.from_mix_project() when Path.expand(root) == File.cwd!(), else nil (Mix.Project describes cwd's project, not an arbitrary root; keeps tmp-root task tests behaviorally unchanged). Pass into SpecLedEx.branch_check(index, root, base: opts[:base], context: context). BranchCheck and Orchestrator need ZERO changes (the :context opt path already exists end-to-end).
- Specs in this stage: compiler_context.spec.md — ADD from_mix_project (must: only Mix-consulting constructor), ADD default_manifest_path (must: default resolves to sibling .mix/compile.elixir of the app dir; integration canary asserts NON-EMPTY manifest through default derivation), REFINE manifest_fixture_integration statement to require the non-empty assertion (id/priority preserved); mix_tasks.spec.md — ADD check_builds_compile_context (must: spec.check constructs Context via from_mix_project when root==cwd, threads into branch guard; --root elsewhere passes no context). Scenarios + tagged_tests verification covers for all, execute: true.
- CHANGELOG.md (Unreleased): merge-on-flush + ghost filtering + context wiring; explicit consumer note: implementation-tier closures now walk the full in-project graph so committed implementation hashes CHANGE — consumers must re-seed (delete implementation section of .spec/realization_hashes.json, run mix spec.check once on a clean tree; Atlas: re-enable tier in .spec/config.yml first). specled_ex's own baseline is api_boundary-only and unaffected.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: mix test test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_test.exs test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_integration_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs; full mix test; mix spec.check
- Behaviors to demonstrate: default-path unit test (tmp tree with fixture-shaped .mix/compile.elixir); from_mix_project in-process (Mix.Project is spec_led_ex in test VM: non-empty manifest containing a known SpecLedEx.* key, compile_path exists); manifest_integration_test STRENGTHENED to map_size(context.manifest) > 0 and Manifest.sources_for(manifest, Sample) != [] via DEFAULT derivation (the assertion that would have caught the ebin/ bug — is_map() alone let an empty map pass); spec_check default_context: %Context{} with non-empty manifest for File.cwd!(), nil for tmp root. Final acceptance run in worktree: mix compile (warm) -> mix spec.check -> mix spec.check again — both green, identical findings (exercises S1+S2+S3 together). New @tag spec: annotations required.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/tracer.ex, lib/specled_ex/compiler/tracer/** (S1)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/** (S2)
- .spec/specs/compiler_tracer.spec.md, .spec/specs/implementation_tier.spec.md
- mix.exs, .spec/config.yml
Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/context.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex
- .spec/specs/compiler_context.spec.md
- .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md
- test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_integration_test.exs
- test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs
- CHANGELOG.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT enable the implementation tier in specled_ex's own .spec/config.yml (consumer-side acceptance, D9c).
- No behavior change for --root <elsewhere> invocations (context stays nil).
- load/1 must remain Mix-free (preserves specled.compiler_context.load_from_opts and mechanical greps in manifest_test.exs).
- spec.check.ex touch impacts api_boundary/branch_guard/mix_tasks surfaces: mix_tasks.spec.md co-changes here; the other two should attest clean via their Mix.Tasks.Spec.Check.run/1 bindings. If a gate run still flags them, co-change those spec files minimally in this stage — do NOT use a severity trailer (trailers go inert once merged to main).

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors
- [ ] mix test
- [ ] mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused
- [ ] mix spec.check
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: a7c01b31-1832-4731-b697-52202d55f59c
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/a7c01b31-1832-4731-b697-52202d55f59c.jsonl

Commit: 7b73e0d (base: S2's 4538bd1)
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/compiler/context.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex, .spec/specs/compiler_context.spec.md, .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md, test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_test.exs, test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_integration_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs, CHANGELOG.md, .spec/state.json (regen)
Tests added: default-manifest-path unit tests (real manifest copied into a tmp app-dir tree; explicit compile_path override finds its own sibling .mix/), from_mix_project in-process tests (non-empty manifest containing SpecLedEx.Parser, compile_path exists, keyword override wins), manifest_integration strengthened to map_size > 0 + sources_for(Sample) != [] via the DEFAULT derivation (the assertion that would have caught the ebin/ bug), default_context/1 tests (cwd -> %Context{} with non-empty manifest; relative-dot root -> context; tmp root -> nil)
Verification stubs flipped: new tagged_tests blocks (compiler_context: default_manifest_path + from_mix_project; mix_tasks: check_builds_compile_context) added at execute: true

Deviations from ticket text, with rationale:
1. mix_tasks.spec.md got requirement + verification cover but NO scenario — that spec file has no spec-scenarios section at all (~20 must requirements, none with scenarios, validates green); following the file's established structure instead of singling out one requirement.
2. Requirement id is specled.tasks.check_builds_compile_context (the file's ids use the specled.tasks.* prefix, not specled.mix_tasks.*).

Risk check that mattered: wiring a context changes Binding.resolve's resolution path for ALL tiers; verified api_boundary committed hashes are stable through the context path — spec.check green with zero drift findings.

Acceptance run: warm compile then consecutive mix spec.check runs — three consecutive green with byte-identical findings output (5 info-level file-touch findings, two attested clean by the realization machinery itself). One earlier acceptance attempt had a one-off merged-run verification_command_failed, green on all re-runs, output lost to a narrow grep; logged with the day's other one-off in tracking ticket specled_-f98.

Gates: mix compile --warnings-as-errors PASS; mix test 888 tests 3 properties 0 failures; mix format --check-formatted PASS; mix deps.unlock --check-unused PASS; mix spec.check status=pass errors=0.

Summary: mix spec.check now hands the realization tiers a real compile context (Context.from_mix_project/1, root==cwd guarded), fixing the production nil-manifest gap; Context.load/1's default manifest path bug (ebin/.mix, 0 modules) is fixed with the canary strengthened so it cannot silently return; CHANGELOG carries the consumer re-seed note for implementation-tier baselines.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: a7c01b31-1832-4731-b697-52202d55f59c (verifier subagent sidechain of this session)
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/a7c01b31-1832-4731-b697-52202d55f59c.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 9 (8 Allowed + .spec/state.json, tool-managed)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3 (new compiler_context tagged_tests block for default_manifest_path + from_mix_project; pre-existing manifest_fixture_integration block covering the refined statement; new mix_tasks block for check_builds_compile_context), passed: 3 (mix spec.check status=pass errors=0)
- Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement):
  - specled.compiler_context.default_manifest_path: covered by test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_test.exs:83 and :107. Reverting the default to Path.join(compile_path, ".mix/compile.elixir") resolves under ebin/ where no manifest exists in the tmp trees; Manifest.load returns %{} (verified: missing path -> %{}), so map_size(context.manifest) > 0 fails in both tests, plus manifest_integration_test.exs:47 via the fixture's default derivation. The :107 test specifically breaks a dirname-free implementation because build_path is nonexistent and only the compile_path override's sibling .mix/ holds a manifest.
  - specled.compiler_context.from_mix_project: covered by test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_test.exs:134 (non-empty manifest containing SpecLedEx.Parser + File.dir?(compile_path)) and :147. Making from_mix_project ignore keyword overrides fails :147 — the derived real manifest_path would load non-empty, breaking assert context.manifest == %{} for the /nonexistent override.
  - specled.compiler_context.manifest_fixture_integration (refined): covered by test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_integration_test.exs:47 (map_size > 0 + Manifest.sources_for(manifest, Sample) != [] through the DEFAULT derivation). An ebin-resolved default loads 0 modules and fails the map_size assertion. Weakening the canary back to is_map would NOT be silent: manifest_test.exs:83/:107 independently catch the ebin bug with the same map_size assertion.
  - specled.tasks.check_builds_compile_context: covered by test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs:139 (cwd -> %Context{} with map_size > 0; fails if the guard stops constructing or from_mix_project breaks), :149 (relative "." root -> context; fails if the guard compares unexpanded strings), :154 (tmp root -> nil; fails if the root==cwd guard is removed and a context is always built). The "threads it into the branch guard" clause has no dedicated assertion (see Notes) — verified by source trace + acceptance run.
- Source-claim traces:
  - (a) only-Mix-consulting constructor: grep of lib/specled_ex/compiler/context.ex shows Mix.* calls only at lines 82-86, all inside from_mix_project/1; remaining hits are doc prose. load/1's body (lines 47-71) consults no Mix module — the Mix.Compilers.Elixir.read_manifest delegation lives in Manifest per manifest_wraps_stdlib. Mechanical greps in manifest_test.exs:155/:161 (api_boundary.ex, binding.ex Mix-global-free) pass.
  - (b) end-to-end threading: spec.check.ex run/1 calls SpecLedEx.branch_check(index, root, base:, context: default_context(root)) -> lib/specled_ex.ex branch_check/3 -> diffcheck/3 -> BranchCheck.run/3 -> run_realization builds realization_opts with context: Keyword.get(opts, :context) (lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex:204) -> Orchestrator.run_with_attestations reads :context (orchestrator.ex:163) and hands it to every tier via dispatch/run_tier (orchestrator.ex:560-583). Confirmed; BranchCheck and Orchestrator needed and received zero changes in this commit.
  - (c) CHANGELOG consumer claim: HashStore baseline is keyed tier -> MFA -> entry; .spec/realization_hashes.json's only top-level key is "api_boundary"; Orchestrator @default_tiers excludes :implementation and .spec/config.yml has no realization.enabled_tiers. So "delete the implementation section, api_boundary unaffected" matches HashStore.merge's per-tier deep-merge semantics, and specled_ex's own baseline is indeed api_boundary-only.
- Acceptance run (step 9): warm mix compile -> mix spec.check --verbose twice: both exit 0, byte-identical output, status=pass errors=0 with exactly the expected 5 info-level branch findings (two attested clean by realization bindings for lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex). An earlier attempt where I ran spec.check with SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1 in the shell env failed on the second run — root-caused as verifier-side env contamination, not a code defect (see Notes; full output captured).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. .spec/config.yml untouched (no realization: key at all — implementation tier not enabled); --root <elsewhere> gets context nil, matching the pre-wiring Keyword.get(opts, :context) == nil behavior exactly; load/1 remains Mix-free; lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, orchestrator.ex, tracer files, mix.exs all untouched per git diff --name-only. Both IMPLEMENTED deviations check out: mix_tasks.spec.md contains zero spec-scenarios blocks anywhere (grep -c = 0, so no scenario is consistent with the file's structure), and every requirement id in that file uses the specled.tasks.* prefix.
- Project verification: pass. mix compile --warnings-as-errors PASS; mix test 888 tests 3 properties 0 failures; targeted files with --include integration 24 tests 0 failures; mix format --check-formatted PASS; mix deps.unlock --check-unused PASS; mix spec.check status=pass errors=0.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Merged-run failure, full detail (per instructions, captured before re-running; full outputs saved at the session scratchpad speccheck_run1.txt/speccheck_run2.txt): with SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1 exported in my shell, run 1 passed but run 2 produced:
  status=fail errors=1 warnings=0
  [ERROR] specled.mix_tasks verification_command_failed .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md :: Verification command failed: tagged_tests: mix test --include integration --formatter SpecLedEx.TaggedTests.Formatter --formatter ExUnit.CLIFormatter <70-file merged list>
  exit_code=2
  failing tests: /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-tracer-merge-on-flush/test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs:11
  ** (Mix) Spec check failed: 1 validation finding(s)
  Root cause (deterministically reproduced): the merged child `mix test` inherits SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1 from the parent env; spec_check_test.exs:11 asserts a DEFAULT run suppresses [INFO] findings and fails whenever the var is set (SPECLED_SHOW_INFO=1 mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs:11 fails 1/1; without the var, passes). It only fails intermittently under spec.check because spec_check_test.exs:42's after-block calls System.delete_env("SPECLED_SHOW_INFO") VM-globally — seed order decides whether line 11 runs before or after that cleanup. This is very likely the same mechanism behind the implementer's one-off verification_command_failed logged in specled_-f98: not a flake in this commit's code, but pre-existing env-hygiene in Mix.Tasks.Spec.CheckTest (no setup scrub of inherited SPECLED_SHOW_INFO). Advisory: (1) note in specled_-f98 that the trigger is running spec.check with SPECLED_SHOW_INFO exported; (2) consider a setup block deleting/restoring the var. Clean acceptance (env var unset, --verbose flag instead, which does not propagate to child VMs) is green twice, byte-identical.
- Advisory coverage nuance on check_builds_compile_context: the tests pin default_context/1's contract (construct for cwd, nil elsewhere), but no test would fail if the single call-site line `context: default_context(root)` were dropped from run/1's branch_check call — specled_ex's own api_boundary attestation output is identical with a nil context, so the acceptance run would not catch it either. The threading clause is verified here by source trace only. Not gating (the requirement's executable cover exists and passes; the clause is one traced line), but a future in-cwd task-level test asserting the context reaches BranchCheck would close it.
- Minor observation, not a defect: from_mix_project/1 always passes an explicit manifest_path (Mix.Project.manifest_path()/compile.elixir), so the production path never exercises load/1's default derivation; the fixed default matters for direct load/1 callers, which is exactly what the unit + integration tests exercise.
- Harmless noise seen during test runs: "/bin/sh: .../specled_cmd_*.sh: No such file or directory" from pre-existing timeout/cleanup fixtures; 0 failures in all runs.

TEST_CRITIC: FIXED (iteration 1)
Critic session: aa44194f096b17b76 (elixir-test-critic agent)
Blocking findings: 0 (advisory findings applied: 3 of 3)
Files reviewed: test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_test.exs, test/specled_ex/compiler/manifest_integration_test.exs, test/mix/tasks/spec_check_test.exs (S3 diff only)

Applied in follow-up commit ce080c1: (1) default_context tests moved from the file's second module — which is async: true via SpecLedEx.Case's default, a placement the critic caught and I had missed — into the async: false module with an explicit cwd-dependence comment; (2) default-manifest-path tests now use @tag :tmp_dir (per-test auto-cleaned dirs; the manual File.rm_rf! leaked on assertion failure); (3) extracted copy_real_manifest!/1 with an assert File.exists? precondition. Critic affirmed: copying the test VM's real compile.elixir is the drift-robust choice (a hand-built manifest would recreate exactly the custom-format-writer trap manifest_wraps_stdlib forbids); tmp-root -> nil is sufficient for the root!=cwd contract (the branch never inspects root contents); SpecLedEx.Parser is a robust manifest anchor. Test-only changes; all gates re-run green (888 tests, spec.check pass).

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: a7c01b31-1832-4731-b697-52202d55f59c
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/a7c01b31-1832-4731-b697-52202d55f59c.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full stub coverage, full requirement
coverage with falsifiability probes reasoned per requirement, zero mechanical
or intent-level scope violations, all source-claim traces grounded (Mix-global
confinement to from_mix_project, end-to-end context threading, tier-keyed
baseline claim), acceptance run green with byte-identical consecutive
spec.check output, and both documented deviations independently confirmed.
The verifier additionally root-caused the merged-run flake (env leak into the
child VM — recorded on specled_-f98, not a defect in this commit). No auditor
was spawned per selective-audit policy. Post-verify delta is the test-only
critic-advisory commit ce080c1 (TEST_CRITIC: FIXED, 0 blocking findings); all
gates re-run green on the final tree.

title: S3: Production Context wiring + Context.load manifest-path fix + release note
